paper.title_en: Localized zero modes enhance massive Casimir interactions
paper.abstract_en: Massive quantum fields usually mediate Casimir interactions that are exponentially screened beyond their Compton wavelength. We show that an isolated normalizable zero mode in a classical background opens a low-frequency collective channel that bypasses this screening. The effective interaction is universal, controlled only by the zero-mode profile. Kinks and planar domain walls provide robust realizations, with the sine-Gordon/Pöschl--Teller kink as an exactly solvable example. Localized zero modes thus define a generic infrared mechanism for modifying massive vacuum forces.
